excerpt: Foreign to this not so much my medical clinical doctor reaction but I honestly feel when I look at Patients like this that they're making a huge mistake and they're trying their best like all of us to maintain themselves and to feel confident and someone somehow they've got an id…

segments.text: foreign to this not so much my medical clinical doctor reaction but I honestly feel when I look at Patients like this that they're making a huge mistake and they're trying their best like all of us to maintain themselves and to feel confident and someone somehow they've got an idea into their head that something is beautiful that just really isn't and I don't think there's a human on the planet who would look at these sorts of results and think anything other than two things one is that's not natural and two is potentially if you're in that social group you might think it's a status symbol and that's the only way I can explain these things you know how you have certain tribes in Africa who have big plates in their lips and people elongate their necks and it's seen as a high status thing within those communities perhaps there's a psychological driver like that in these communities where that is deemed as

somehow High status but I promise you one thing it is not beautiful if you believe that beauty is a reflection of health and good genetics and all those things that I believe Beauty real Beauty stems from I really hope those patients are happy and it's serving them in some way but I have this horrible feeling that it's not actually creating happiness and even if it is short-term I can tell you with that much product projected particularly onto the chin it's not going to last it'll collapse I think three months down the line you'll have a wildly different result and that that's the other element to it but mainly I just I hope that I wish these people would have more guidance

transcript: foreign to this not so much my medical clinical doctor reaction but I honestly feel when I look at Patients like this that they're making a huge mistake and they're trying their best like all of us to maintain themselves and to feel confident and someone somehow they've got an idea into their head that something is beautiful that just really isn't and I don't think there's a human on the planet who would look at these sorts of results and think anything other than two things one is that's not natural and two is potentially if you're in that social group you might think it's a status symbol and that's the only way I can explain these things you know how you have certain tribes in Africa who have big plates in their lips and people elongate their necks and it's seen as a high status thing within those communities perhaps there's a psychological driver like that in these communities where that is deemed as somehow High status but I promise you one thing it is not beautiful if you believe that beauty is a reflection of health and good genetics and all those things that I believe Beauty real Beauty stems from I really hope those patients are happy and it's serving them in some way but I have this horrible feeling that it's not actually creating happiness and even if it is short-term I can tell you with that much product projected particularly onto the chin it's not going to last it'll collapse I think three months down the line you'll have a wildly different result and that that's the other element to it but mainly I just I hope that I wish these people would have more guidance
